title: About Me
text:
I’m Jennifer Onyebuchi — a social impact leader, data scientist in training, and passionate advocate for digital inclusion and gender equality in technology.
I’m currently pursuing my Master’s in Data Science at the University of British Columbia as a Mastercard Foundation Scholar, where I’m building the skills to become a data scientist who drives equitable change.
My passion lies in harnessing the power of data to bridge inequities — especially in education, technology access, and opportunities for underrepresented groups.

title: About Me
text:
My Mission

Every child deserves the opportunity to thrive in the digital age, regardless of where they are born.

This belief is at the core of everything I do. I founded the Tech Big Sis Foundation to make this a reality — empowering African children and young girls with digital skills and preparing them for the future of work.

Key Initiatives



Initiative
Focus




TEACE — Tech Every African Ch
TEACE �gital literacy for chi

title: About Me
text:
Beyond the Work
When I’m not wrangling data or running programmes, I enjoy mentoring emerging leaders, public speaking, and finding creative intersections between technology and social good.
See My Projects →

title: Jennifer Ezinne Onyebuchi
text:
Data Scientist

Hi! I’m Jennifer, with a background in data science and social impact leadership. I’m passionate about how data, dashboards, predictive and analytical insights translate into real world decisions that shape everyday lives and create equitable outcomes. I am interested in harnessing AI and technology to bridge inequities — especially for underrepresented communities across Africa.
View Projects Contact Me

title: Canadian Migrant Education Analysis
text: ← Back to Projects

title: Canadian Migrant Education Analysis
text:
Overview
An interactive data visualization Shiny app exploring migrant education trends in Canada. The app enables users to filter and compare indicators across regions and time, making it easier to uncover patterns in access, participation, and outcomes.

title: Canadian Migrant Education Analysis
text:
The Problem
Migrant students in Canada face unique barriers in education — language, mobility, and socioeconomic challenges. But data about their experiences is scattered and hard to explore. This project makes that data accessible and explorable for researchers, educators, and policymakers.

title: Canadian Migrant Education Analysis
text:
What I Built

An interactive Shiny dashboard that visualises migrant education indicators across Canadian provinces
Filters by region, time period, and education level
Visual storytelling using Plotly and Altair charts to surface key patterns

title: Canadian Migrant Education Analysis

title: Vancouver Food Finder
text: ← Back to Projects

title: Vancouver Food Finder
text:
Overview
A dashboard helping Vancouver residents experiencing food insecurity find free or low-cost food supports — including meal programs and food hampers — in their area.

title: Vancouver Food Finder
text:
The Problem
Many food support programs exist across Vancouver, but information about them is scattered and difficult to search or compare. People in need often can’t easily find what’s available near them, what it costs, or what access requirements exist.

title: Vancouver Food Finder
text:
What I Built

An interactive map-based dashboard using Kepler.gl and ipyleaflet to show program locations across Vancouver
Filters by service type, cost, and access requirements
Built with the City of Vancouver Food Programs open dataset which includes both program details and geospatial information
